LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS PETER WELTER AND FERNANDO MIRANDA'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF MICHAEL D. SHORE'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING LIABILITY FOR CONTRIBUTION DUE TO LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION AND/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO DISMISS MICHAEL D. SHORE'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING LIABILITY FOR CONTRIBUTION DUE TO FORUM NON CONVENIENS - MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED AS TO WELTER BUT DENIED AS TO MIRANDA WHOSE ON GOING CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA ARE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT GENERAL JURISDICTION. THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST AS TO FORUM NON-CONVENIENS. EVEN ASSUMING THAT FORUM NON-CONVENIENS IS APPROPRIATE AS TO THIS KIND OF MOTION, IT WILL DEPRIVE MR. SHORE OF HIS CA FORUM FOR A DETERMINATION OF WHETHER THE AMOUNT HE HAD TO PAY BASED ON THE SISTER STATE JUDGMENT IN CA SHOULD OR SHOULD NOT BE SHARED AMONG OTHERS. THIS IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE ON THE HEARING OF THE UNDERLYING MOTION BEFORE COMMISSIONER HEWLETT. MR. JAMES IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN CSR# 8189 301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Motin To Quash Service Of Michael D. Shore'S Motion For Order Determining Liability For Contribution Due To Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And/Or In The Alternative Motion To Dismiss Michael D. Shore'S Motion For Order Determining Liability For Contribution Due To Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Feb-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL P. IANNITELLI OF THE MCGARY FIRM, TEL. NO. 874-3500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. H. NATHAN JAMES OF THE LAW FIRM OF SEIFER, MURKEN, & TEICHMAN, TEL. NO. 749-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS PETER WELTER AND FERNANDO MIRANDA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Michael D. Shore'S Motion For Order Determining Liability For Contribution Due To Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And/Or In The Alternative Motion To Dismiss Michael D. Shore'S Motion For Order Determining Liability For Contribution Due To Forum Non Conveniens - MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED AS TO WELTER BUT DENIED AS TO MIRANDA WHOSE ON GOING CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA ARE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT GENERAL JURISDICTION. THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST AS TO FORUM NON-CONVENIENS. EVEN ASSUMING THAT FORUM NON-CONVENIENS IS APPROPRIATE AS TO THIS KIND OF MOTION, IT WILL DEPRIVE MR. SHORE OF HIS CA FORUM FOR A DETERMINATION OF WHETHER THE AMOUNT HE HAD TO PAY BASED ON THE SISTER STATE JUDGMENT IN CA SHOULD OR SHOULD NOT BE SHARED AMONG OTHERS. THIS IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE ON THE HEARING OF THE UNDERLYING MOTION BEFORE COMMISSIONER HEWLETT. MR. JAMES is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol A. Karen CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/ER =301/PJB/ER
